title: DORA Post-Incident Review and Learning

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.13 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.13 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.3.10: DORA Post-Incident Review and Learning.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.10' running on notable macro, index=itsm (PIR/RCA records), dora_pir_completion.csv (KV store), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") status="Closed" earliest=-180d
| lookup dora_pir_completion.csv notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT pir_status, pir_date, root_cause, improvement_actions, actions_completed
| eval pir_due=if(isnull(pir_status), "MISSING", pir_status)
| eval actions_closed=if(isnotnull(actions_completed) AND actions_completed="yes", "DONE", "OPEN")
| stats count by pir_due, actions_closed
| append [
    search `notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") status="Closed" earliest=-180d
    | lookup dora_pir_completion.csv notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT pir_status, improvement_actions, actions_completed
    | where pir_status!="completed" OR isnull(pir_status) OR actions_completed!="yes"
    | table _time, rule_name, urgency, pir_status, improvement_actions, actions_completed
    | sort - _time
]
description: Article 13 requires financial entities to have capabilities and staff to learn from ICT-related incidents, share lessons, and evolve their ICT risk management framework. This use case tracks whether major incidents result in completed post-incident reviews (PIRs), that root causes are documented, and that improvement actions are implemented ‚Äî providing the "learning and evolving" evidence DORA specifically mandates.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Create `dora_pir_completion.csv` KV store linking ES notable IDs to PIR records; (2) require PIR completion within 30 days of incident closure; (3) track root cause categories for trend analysis; (4) monitor improvement action implementation status; (5) report on learning cycle completion rates to management body; (6) share anonymised lessons across business units as Art. 13 requires. Indexes required: `index=itsm`.
visualization: Pie chart (PIR completion status), Table (incidents without PIR), Bar chart (root cause categories), Single value (PIR completion rate %), Timeline (PIR lifecycle). Key fields: `actions_closed`, `actions_completed`, `improvement_actions`, `pir_due`, `pir_status`, `rule_name`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for article 13 requires financial entities to have capabilities and staff to learn from ict-related incidents, share lessons, and evolve their ict risk management framework. this use case tracks whethe. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` (PIR/RCA records), `dora_pir_completion.csv` (KV store).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Create `dora_pir_completion.csv` KV store linking ES notable IDs to PIR records; (2) require PIR completion within 30 days of incident closure; (3) track root cause categories for trend analysis; (4) monitor improvement action implementation status; (5) report on learning cycle completion rates to management body; (6) share anonymised lessons across business units as Art. 13 requires.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") status="Closed" earliest=-180d
| lookup dora_pir_completion.csv notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT pir_status, pir_date, root_cause, improvement_actions, actions_completed
| eval pir_due=if(isnull(pir_status), "MISSING", pir_status)
| eval actions_closed=if(isnotnull(actions_completed) AND actions_completed="yes", "DONE", "OPEN")
| stats count by pir_due, actions_closed
| append [
    search `notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") status="Closed" earliest=-180d
    | lookup dora_pir_completion.csv notable_id AS event_id OUTPUT pir_status, improvement_actions, actions_completed
    | where pir_status!="completed" OR isnull(pir_status) OR actions_completed!="yes"
    | table _time, rule_name, urgency, pir_status, improvement_actions, actions_completed
    | sort - _time
]
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Post-Incident Review and Learning (Art. 13)** ‚Äî Article 13 requires financial entities to have capabilities and staff to learn from ICT-related incidents, share lessons, and evolve their ICT risk management framework. This use case tracks whether major incidents result in completed post-incident reviews (PIRs), that root causes are documented, and that improvement actions are implemented ‚Äî providing the "learning and evolving" evidence DORA specifically mandates.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` macro, `index=itsm` (PIR/RCA records), `dora_pir_completion.csv` (KV store). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Invokes macro `notable` ‚Äî in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **pir_due** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **actions_closed** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by pir_due, actions_closed** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Appends rows from a subsearch with `append`.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Pie chart (PIR completion status), Table (incidents without PIR), Bar chart (root cause categories), Single value (PIR completion rate %), Timeline (PIR lifecycle).
